CMS Conditions of Participation

The Centers for Medicare & Medicaid Services (CMS) Conditions of Participation (CoPs) are health and safety standards that healthcare organizations must meet in order to begin and continue participating in the Medicare and Medicaid programs.

Hospital CoPs Overview

Hospitals must comply with CMS CoPs to receive reimbursement under the Medicare program (42 CFR Part 482). These regulations cover areas such as patient rights, nursing services, medical staff qualifications, infection control, and quality improvement programs.

Key Requirements

  • Patient Rights (42 CFR 482.13): Hospitals must inform patients of their rights.
  • Medical Staff (42 CFR 482.22): Physicians must be credentialed and privileged.
  • Nursing Services (42 CFR 482.23): Registered nurses must supervise nursing care.
  • Infection Control (42 CFR 482.42): Hospitals must maintain an infection control program.
  • Quality Assessment (42 CFR 482.21): Hospitals must maintain a QAPI program.

Regulatory References

These conditions are published in the Code of Federal Regulations (CFR) Title 42. Compliance is verified through the State Survey Agency and CMS regional offices. ICD-10-CM codes and CPT codes are used to document care provided under these conditions.
